other_observers: Cole Wild & Nick Komar; I found the bird
description: A medium-small hummingbird dominated by green back, grayish underparts, and a few (3, I believe) central gorget feathers. The bill was of moderate length and nearly straight. The crown was fairly bright green (considering the poor light, with the green coloration continuing down the back. The face featured a white post-occular spot, but no white above or in front of the eye. A prominent gray auricular contrasted with the whitish throat, which featured rows of fine dark spots and the aforementioned iridescent red gorget feathers that would go from black through pink and ruby-red depending on the angle. The rest of the underparts were light gray, a bit darker on the flanks than on the breast or belly. The tail was deeply notches, with the wingtips falling well short of the tail tip. I didn't obtain good views of the primary shapes.Behaviors: The bird was feeding on agastache flowers and perching to rest in between feeding bouts. When hovering to feed, no tail flicking was observed beyond that expected for flight control.Call: Mostly silent but a few faint smacking chips heard.Plumage: Immature Male
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
